reviews.review_description:
Based on the recent reviews, I was expecting the game not to be good, but to my surprise, I really like it.

I just bought it yesterday, on the sale, and I actually went through the tutorial to orient myself.

It has little short videos.  The first one is Billy Blanks showing you technique.  I remember him from "back in the day", so it was nice to see him.

The next video shows you how to practice the technique in game.

Last night I did the punch, jab, and hooks (right and left).

I also practiced with the punching bag.

The music was not the latest hit or anything, but it was also not annoying like a lot of royalty-free music used to be (of course now with AI there is no excuse for bad game music, but I digress).

I can see myself really learning a lot of technique and getting in shape too with this app.

I work remotely and mostly do strength training at home. It's winter and it's cold outside. When I need to recover from my actual training, games like these are a great way to increase activity and calorie burn and get blood flowing in a fun way without increasing recovery time.
Taebo Reboot is not as high quality as some similar other well-known titles but it's okay.  Music is pretty bad and often annoying and there are many exploits, such as the walls that should require the player to slip or side-step don't actually come in the way at all if you just stand straight in the middle. 
The coins and money bags seem to not add anything gameplay-wise and it feels pointless to collect them. The "speedballs" are often at eye level and block you from seeing what color the next one is if you don't awkwardly lean to the other side while punching.
It's in early access but update changelog or roadmap is nowhere to be found so I can't be sure if it's abandoned already. Meta should require EA devs to push updates on a regular basis and communicate the changes to players.

developer_response.body:
Hey FlirtyRandomizer. Thanks a lot for taking the time to write such a detailed and honest review. We genuinely appreciate it.

Regarding quality, music, and exploits: your feedback is valid. Taebo Reboot is still in Early Access, and we are actively working on improving overall polish, balance, and gameplay integrity. Issues like wall obstacles not properly enforcing movement, speedballs blocking visibility, and exploits that reduce challenge are all things we’re aware of and are addressing internally. These are not abandoned systems, they are actively being iterated on.

For the coins and money bags, you’re also right that their current gameplay value feels limited. They are part of systems that are still being developed and expanded, and their role will become more meaningful as progression, rewards, and meta gameplay evolve.

On music, we hear this feedback a lot. Audio is one of the areas scheduled for improvement, including better variety, tone, and overall experience so it feels motivating rather than distracting.

As for communication, that’s fair criticism. The project is absolutely still in development, but we agree we need to do a better job sharing roadmaps, changelogs, and upcoming improvements with players. This is something we’re actively working on so Early Access players have clearer visibility on what’s coming and what’s being fixed.

We really appreciate constructive feedback like yours. It helps us prioritize the right improvements and make Taebo Reboot a better experience over time. Thanks for supporting us

reviews.review_title: I love this game so far
reviews.review_description: I have been out of shape due to a prolonged illness and I am trying to get back into fitness. I remember my marine friend mentioned Mr. Billy Blank's Taboe from back in the day. So, when I stumbled upon this jewel of a game with no subscription I was sold. It is well made game and meant for serious fitness as a previous reviewer stated. But it is also something you can built on I think. It's challenging enough to motivate you and not too challenging to make ya just quit. I like the mini games. I would suggest maybe a treadmill one and a music rhythm one with lights you can punch to a beat. Now this might be a bit far fetched suggestion but if I am not wrong Mr. Blank is a practioner of Wing Chung. So maybe a wooden dummy mini game too. Sorry , I am just excited about the possibilities and I love this game so far. Thank you for creating this and cant wait for future additions to it. I am still curious about the store and if there is anyone ever in his office.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for this amazing message. We are really glad Taebo Reboot is helping you get back into fitness after everything you have been through. Billy Blanks has inspired so many people over the years and it means a lot to us to hear that the VR version is doing the same for you today. We love your ideas too. More mini games will definitely come in future updates and we enjoy hearing creative suggestions like yours. Thanks again for the support and the enthusiasm. We are excited to keep building Taebo Reboot with the community and we hope you will enjoy everything we add next.

reviews.review_title: Just right for a father of a 2 yo
reviews.review_description: I wasn’t expecting this but here we go. Beat saber worked great for me because I can put the headset, play a couple of songs and be good for another diaper change and running session with my kid. Most exercising games take it way too serious or long, trying to get the players to stay as long as possible. Here we have small  games that you can focus on and complete and then move on with your life. It’s refreshing and removes the feeling of missing out that I sometimes get when I have to cancel a season before completing it.

Meta Quest 3 version. Got it in sale and there is some promise for that Gen X taebo fix, but the bugs in audio, video, game play...just kill it. 

Good idea just not there yet.

reviews.review_title: U must jump in your appartment
reviews.review_description:
such a nogo, jumping in an appartment. My neighbours knocked already on my door.  plz remove jumping or make an optin to disable it. Jumping srarts at fundumentals 7.
The hit counter on the dummy is extremly bad when it comes down to hooks...
i dont get the combo master, it always says incorrect.
plz do add a burned kcal counter like in les mills body combat. And a counter for daily weekly monthly and yearly burned kcal like in body combat.
plz akso ,ake a support discord for feedback and so like many other apps do.

developer_response.body: Thanks for trying the early access version of Taebo Reboot. We appreciate you taking the time to share your feedback. The early access build is still evolving and many features you mentioned will be added or improved as we move toward the 2026 release. This includes options for jumps, better hit detection, clearer tutorials for the Combo Master, a full system with daily and yearly stats, and a dedicated community support channel. Your comments help us shape the experience and we hope that when you come back in future updates you will enjoy the progress and give us five stars.

reviews.review_title: I am too old for this
reviews.review_description:
I use my Quest 3 purely for getting in about 30 minutes of a workout after a 12-hour workday. VR eliminates the time (that I don't have) for driving to and from the gym. I was really excited when I found out about Tae Bo: Reboot. I remembered the commercials from the '80s and was expecting a similar experience.

This is not that.

The app consists of small, bite-sized levels and 2-3 minute sessions packed with a lot of variation in moves, some not seen elsewhere, presented in a gamified experience. It has the celebrity factor—Billy Blanks' voice and likeness are everywhere—but it lacks some polish. The audio is inconsistent, with some comments sounding clear and others muffled. The graphics are nice, but there is too much focus on it being a game rather than a "fitness app."

Collecting coins and experience points to "level up" is a cool and unique concept for this type of game, but getting a "fail" after completing a workout is not very encouraging. The bombs definitely do not help; getting an instant death from being hit by an object in a fitness app is a no-go for me. Without that element, the "levels" are challenging, but even at the beginner/tutorial stages, things move and change too quickly. The challenge is there, but it seems it's more of a "trick you" challenge rather than the challenge of a good workout.

Suggestions/Requests:

Please remove the bombs.

Make the objects semi-transparent so you can see the next one or two objects and prepare yourself. Even at the beginner levels, there are portions where you only have a split second to completely change your arm direction.

The walls need to be clearer. I play in XR mode, and the walls are hard to see when rendered over real-life objects.

Include workout routines like the VHS tapes of the golden age. I was hoping the TV in the game would offer something like that, but it does not.

Being able to "participate" in an actual Tae Bo class, whether recorded or in-game engine style, would be absolutely amazing.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for taking the time to write such a thoughtful and detailed review. Your feedback carries a lot of weight for us, especially coming from someone who uses VR for consistent daily workouts. We truly appreciate hearing from players who push the app the way it is meant to be used.

You brought up excellent points. The audio inconsistency is already on our improvement list. We are reworking the entire voice pipeline with Billy so that every line is clear, balanced and consistent. Regarding the overall feel leaning more toward a game than a fitness app, we are actively fine tuning that balance so the fun elements never interfere with the quality of the workout.

About the bombs, we hear you. This feature has been polarizing and we are currently testing new approaches, including removing them in certain modes. Your comment reinforces the need to adjust that part of the experience.

Your suggestions about object visibility are also spot on. We are improving transparency, timing readability and wall clarity, especially for XR players, so you can anticipate movements better without feeling rushed or surprised.

And yes, the classic full length Tae Bo style routines are something many players want. We are exploring ways to bring longer structured sessions into the game, either through recorded content or using the in game engine to simulate a proper class. This idea fits perfectly with what the community is asking for.

Thanks again for taking the time to share all of this. Feedback like yours genuinely helps shape the future of Tae Bo Reboot and we are working hard to make the experience match what dedicated players like you are looking for.

Wishing you solid workouts ahead and thanks for being part of the journey.

reviews.review_description: This game is well done for what it is. Boxing is what I enjoy most when working out. But I, personally,  need something more competitive, where I can box against someone or with other people that I can accumulate more points and win. Im sure this is a great fitness game just not one for me.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for giving TaeBo Reboot a try and for explaining your thoughts so clearly. It is always helpful to hear from players who enjoy boxing as their main workout style, because that perspective really matters to us.

I completely understand where you are coming from. Some people are driven by competition, direct matchups, leaderboards, or the feeling of going head to head with other players. TaeBo Reboot is built more around solo progression and personal improvement, so if you are looking specifically for competitive sparring or versus style workouts, I can see why this one might not fully click.

Your comment still means a lot, because it highlights an important player segment that we want to support better in the future. Competitive features like leaderboards and community challenges are things we plan to expand as the game evolves. While it may not change the core direction right away, it is absolutely something we take seriously.

Thank you again for trying it out and for sharing your honest thoughts. Wishing you great workouts with the boxing experiences that fire you up the most.

reviews.review_description:
This is an awesome rhythm style boxing workout game! Each of the fundamentals are taught by Billy himself demonstrating form, and the game mechanics are broken down and introduced in small segments that reward completion of the learning process. I will say though, there are a lot to get through so don’t expect to jump straight into the main gameplay right away. 

The environments and art style are simple but fun and engaging, and I really love how targets fly off directionally with the impact you put in. It feels extremely satisfying and engaging. Each of the books have a totally different theme and environment and it keeps things fun and fresh.

After mastering the fundamentals, you have four elemental books and one master book, each with twenty challenges, and there are also mini games / training exercises in the lobby. 

I get a fantastic and fun workout, and I can tell I’ll be putting lots of time into this one.  Besides working out your body, you will have some serious reflex training with the quick reaction time required and continually tracking and hitting moving targets. 

That being said, there are a few downsides. This game is a serious fitness game, and it doesn’t really have accessibility features. You will be required to jump, duck, and dodge constantly, and you can’t turn those off in settings. You can enable “god mode” to get through the workouts, but that stops you from tracking progress and achievements. What’s even weirder is despite this requiring a serious fitness commitment, it also feels too gamified. There are many different mechanics and your brain has to be constantly giving just as much as your body in order to keep up with all the mechanics. I wish that these too could be turned on/off with an adjustment to score instead of just straight up not tracking anything to progress. Just something to know before going in. 

All that being said, this is day 1, and this is a serious and super fun workout game and a great reflex trainer as well.

developer_response.body: Really appreciate the detailed feedback! Great to hear you’re enjoying the fundamentals, environments, and that satisfying feel of hitting the targets, that’s exactly the kind of experience we hoped to deliver. You’re right that it’s a serious workout, and we also hear you on the accessibility side. Those features are almost ready and will be included in the next update. Thanks again for taking the time to write this and for jumping in on day 1!💪🥊

reviews.review_title: This is great app with great workouts
reviews.review_description: I was a die hard TaeBo’er in my 20’s. This takes me back! I love all of the features. Got our 11 year old son doing it and I just gave it a try. It’s a super cool feeling like you’re right inside the gym and Dojo. Definitely worth a try!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for this amazing review. It means a lot to us to hear from someone who loved Tae Bo in the early days. We are really happy that Taebo Reboot brings back that feeling and that your son is enjoying it too. Our goal is to recreate the energy of the gym and the dojo in a fresh VR experience and your message tells us we are on the right track. Thanks again for the support and enjoy your training.

reviews.review_description:
I bought this game because I often play LES MILLS for my workout, but wanted something a little different.
While some of the mechanics are the same, there is enough variety to add to my workout.

Looking forward to the full release, and curious if there will be other minigames and more types of workouts.
I tried liteboxing vr before it because subscription based, and trying to find a non-subscription game similar to that (closest is punch fit)

Ideally I would love to see a mode focused on form, because while I enjoy the game itself, just like LES MILLS, it sometimes goes a little too fast that you have to sacrifice form in order to hit the target.

Maybe a mode where it is similar to the old taebo series, seeing that there are video clips of it in the background.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for taking the time to share your experience. It is great to hear from someone who uses LES MILLS regularly and was looking for something fresh to add to their workout routine. I am glad TaeBo Reboot brought you a bit of variety while still feeling familiar enough to fit into your training flow.

Your comments about speed and form are very appreciated. You are absolutely right. When things ramp up, it can sometimes feel like you need to choose between proper technique and hitting the targets. We are already working on ideas for additional modes that focus more on clean form and slower paced precision, so players can train technique without sacrificing the fun. Hearing your request reinforces how important this direction is.

You also mentioned interest in more minigames and workout types. That is something we are actively planning for full release and beyond. The goal is to give players multiple ways to stay engaged, whether they want a full class style session, short intense challenges, or something more structured like the old TaeBo tapes you mentioned. We love that you noticed the background clips, and you are not the only one hoping for a mode inspired by those classics.

Thanks as well for bringing up subscription free alternatives. We know how important it is for many users to have a strong fitness app without long term recurring costs, so we are doing our best to offer something meaningful in a one time purchase.

Really appreciate your thoughtful review. It helps us shape the future of TaeBo Reboot in the best way possible. If you stick around for the full release, we hope to deliver exactly the kind of options you are looking for.

reviews.review_description:
Taebo Reboot is hands-down one of the best VR fitness games out there! It delivers an incredibly challenging workout that gets your heart pumping while being super fun and engaging—perfect for staying motivated during sessions. The gameplay feels dynamic and addictive, blending intense Tae Bo-style punching with satisfying progression.

I'm really looking forward to the developers adding a global leaderboard (or even friend-based ones) so we can compare scores and push each other further—that would take it to the next level!

That said, there are a couple of frustrating bugs holding me back: in the Level 3 of the "Book of Emptiness," the targets never appear, making it impossible to complete. Same issue with Level 20 in the last book—no targets spawn, so I can't progress. Hoping for a quick patch on those!

Overall, highly recommended for anyone wanting a top-notch VR cardio blast. 9/10

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this powerful review. It is awesome to hear that TaeBo Reboot is giving you the kind of intense and motivating workout you were hoping for. When someone describes the game as both challenging and addictive, that tells us we are on the right track with the mix of fitness and progression.

Your comment about leaderboards made me smile because that is one of the most requested features from our competitive players. A global board or even a friends only board would definitely elevate the motivation factor, and it is something the team wants to bring in when the time is right. You are absolutely right that it would push people further.

Now about those bugs you mentioned. Thank you for calling them out clearly. The missing targets in Level three of the Book of Emptiness and Level twenty in the final book are known issues and our team is already working on them. These glitches block progression and we take that seriously. A fix is on the way and we really appreciate your patience.

Your nine out of ten rating means a lot. Reviews like yours help the game grow while also encouraging the team behind it to keep improving.

Thanks again for the support and the positivity. Hope your next sessions are even stronger once those bugs are patched.

reviews.review_description: This is one of the best workout VR apps I've used, maybe the best.  You progress through levels that get rapidly more difficult.  Lots of punching, bobbing, weaving, jumping.  There is also a tutorial mode that goes through each of the moves individually and gradually adds the moves on top of each other such as different punching methods onto a target that flies at you also introducing spiked mines that fly at you you must dodge.  The video introductions by Billy Blanks showing you the stances and movements are incredibly helpful and well done.  The reason I am not giving it 5 stars is I cannot use it.  I have a bad hip and cannot jump.  There is no way in the levels to be able to remove certain movements that are required.  So the short wall that comes at you that is need to jump up to avoid is always there so I get a big, fat Fail on screen.  It's kinda disheartening to be working out for health and get fail slammed at you constantly.  I was able to use the gym mode where you pick one exercise to focus on but that left the main part of the game I wasn't doing.  There is a lot of movement in this game so make sure you have a generous play area.  It is a very polished app.

developer_response.body: Thanks for laying it out in detail. It means a lot to hear you rate the game that highly, even with the frustration around the jump walls. That’s valuable feedback, accessibility and movement options are things we’re looking at for future updates. Glad the tutorials and Billy’s guidance helped, and we’ll work to make the rest more flexible too.

reviews.review_description:
This is decent. Compared to Les Mills it is a bit more of a focus and coordination/speed game. I like the variations / movement in targets, and especially that there aren't any moves that the quest has trouble tracking (T-strikes, ground pounds etc). 

Cons
The hits are not sync'd with the music, though it seems like they start that way and are meant to be (?). This bothers me a lot but may not bother others. The music is kinda underwhelming video game music (so far). Probably better to put in phones. It also seems power is not rewarded in any obvious way. The pads fly away about the same whether I hit them hard or soft. At later levels the pads come faster than good form allows so I basically end up shrugging and walking away.

Still, gets you moving and a bit different than LM. I am gonna return this one for now. I prefer more structure toward exercise with form, less toward swatting wildly at a swarm of wasps.

developer_response.body:
I really appreciate you taking the time to share such an honest and balanced perspective. It means a lot to us when someone gives clear reasoning rather than just a simple thumbs up or down, so thank you for that.

You make several strong points. You are right about the focus on coordination and speed. Tae Bo Reboot leans heavily into movement variety and reaction timing, and we know this creates a different feel compared to a structured fitness format like Les Mills. We are actively working on improving that balance so the workout side feels just as strong as the gameplay side.

The music timing note is completely valid. Some tracks currently align well with patterns while others need refinement. Our team is already reviewing the audio sync system to make the rhythm feel more connected to your punches. The music itself is also being expanded and upgraded, with new tracks and stronger energy planned to match the pacing of the workouts.

You also mentioned the hit power not affecting the pads enough. That is an important detail, and we are adjusting the power feedback system so hard strikes feel more meaningful and satisfying. It should always feel like proper form and strong technique matter.

Your point about higher levels speeding up beyond good form is also something we are tuning. We want challenge, but not at the cost of proper technique. More structured training paths and optional modes with a fitness focus are in development.

I understand completely if you choose to return the app for now. Even so, your feedback is genuinely valuable to us, and it helps guide the improvements we are already working on.

Thank you again for giving it a fair try and for explaining your thoughts so clearly. If you ever check back in the future, I hope you will find an experience that matches exactly what you are looking for.

reviews.review_description:
Ok, as someone who is always looking for a new workout and has used most VR fitness apps, here are my comments.  I use the app for fitness not playing it as a game.

I.  I give it 4 stars mainly because I don’t give new games less than 4 unless they are just utterly tone deaf as to how bad they are.  

I just don’t think it’s fair to the game developers as they haven’t had enough time to react to first impressions.


2.  My “real” rating would probably be 3. 


Plusses:

Workout Variety and Dynamics

There were some different things that I liked. Moving targets for one.

Jumping is a dynamic I’ve been wanting to see.

￼Different dodge and blocking elements.


Number of Workouts:

There are enough to get you started.  And they have a good bit of variety.


Gaming:

Unlike other workout apps there is a good bit of gaming going on here.  It’s not something I really care about too much but adds a little bit of interest.

————-


Minuses:

Marketing & Number of Workouts: 

It says over 100 workouts but each workout is only about 3 mins so…. Not sure if that is misleading or not, but in general doesn’t a workout need to be at least 15 mins to do much of anything?   If you are trying to get a full 30 mins of cardio there are probably maybe 16 or so full workouts?  I could be wrong, but the first level has about 30 3 mins workouts and there are 5 levels.

Coaching: 

As others have mentioned, we get either an AI coach or just little quips.  “Stay focused!”   

Honestly I think I would rather have nothing. 

My feedback for the Devs would be to use AI to try to create a more realistic coach.  I know it’s possible.  

I’ve heard AI phone answering apps that are so realistic sounding and respond on the fly, so I do think this could be better.  Don’t you have years of original taebo tapes to train on?


Music: 

The music was kind of meaningless.  Not anything I would ever choose to workout to.  Felt a bit too Arcade-like.  I didn’t hate it, but it didn’t do anything for me.  

Overall I do recommend it.  Looking forward to updates though.

developer_response.body:
We really appreciate you taking the time to write such a detailed and thoughtful review. It’s clear you’ve spent time exploring what Taebo Reboot offers, and your perspective as someone focused on fitness more than gaming is incredibly valuable to us.

You’re right about the current workout length and the early structure. The short three-minute challenges are designed for quick bursts of intensity, but we agree that combining them into longer sequences will make it easier to reach a full 20 to 30-minute cardio session. That’s already in our roadmap as we build the full program progression.

As for coaching, we completely hear you. The goal is to bring Billy’s real energy and guidance into the experience in a more dynamic way. We’re experimenting with AI-assisted voice interaction and better contextual feedback to make the coaching feel more natural and responsive.

Music is another area we’re evolving fast. You’ll start hearing more variety, stronger beats, and thematic styles that better match the environments and intensity of each workout.

Your honesty helps us improve exactly where it matters most. Thanks for giving it a try and for believing in what we’re building. We’ve got big updates ahead and can’t wait to show you what’s next. 💪

reviews.review_description: As a big fan of Billy Blanks back in the day, I am excited to say the workouts in Taebo are awesome.  the fact that all this content is available with no Subscription is a home run.

developer_response.body: Thank you Todd for the great review. We are really happy to hear that the workouts bring back the classic Billy Blanks energy you enjoyed back in the day. Knowing that fans like you are enjoying Taebo Reboot means a lot to our team. Offering full workouts with no subscription is something we truly believe in and your message tells us it matters. Thanks again for the support and enjoy your training.

reviews.review_description:
Kick, punch its all in the mind! (Maybe no the kicking bit XD)

Taebo Reboot is a super fun arcady style exercise app that will have you ducking weaving jumping and of course punching! 

Set across a series of books each with 20 challenges the game start you of slowly before really ramping up the difficulty by the end.

There is so much content to exercise to with most levels having their own unique music track.

There are some added bonus exercise apparatus to test yourself against Billy.

Whist's currently the game only has local leaderboards (where you can have multiple profiles for the family)

The developer has already said online leader boards will be coming eventually

Im a couch potato normally but I thoroughly enjoyed working my way through the extensive number of levels (currently 140 levels) and I've still got some to go (they are out of my current capabilities XD)

Overall a very fun fitness based game that actually will appeal to fans of rhythm games too.

The developer still aren't finished with the game (there an in game store coming soon and the mentioned online leaderboards)
But its already such an extensive package that it can be easily for given.

Can't wait to see if the developer continues to expand on the base game with more books and levels (they have something really good here already!)

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for this awesome write-up! Glad to hear you’re having fun working through all the challenges (and yes, they definitely ramp up fast). We really appreciate you noticing the variety of content already in there, and you’re right, online leaderboards and the in-game store are on the way. We’ve got more updates planned too, so hopefully we’ll keep surprising you with fresh books and levels. Thanks again for the support! 🥊💪

reviews.review_description: The visuals in each different setting is stunning and I wish it were a bit more interactive where I could leave the challenge for a few minutes to explore the scenery.  Each segment you work through offers a different level of activity but I miss the music that gives Billy Blanks in-studio feel when I used to attend classes in person.  The gym is nice and I look forward to a bit more interactive movement around the gym. I love that it tracks my progress and I can actually understand where I need improvement.  It's a lot to learn but Taebo was never for the fainthearted.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing such a thoughtful impression of your experience. It is wonderful to hear that the visuals made an impact on you. The environments are a big part of the atmosphere we tried to create, and knowing that they made you want to explore them more is a huge compliment. The idea of being able to step out of the challenge and look around freely is something we have heard from a few players, and it is definitely on our creative wishlist for the future.

Your comment about the music really hits home. Billy’s in studio energy from the classic sessions is something a lot of longtime Tae Bo fans remember with a ton of affection. We are working toward bringing more musical personality and presence into the experience so it captures that same feeling you described.

The gym and the desire for more interaction are also great points. Expanding the hub and allowing more movement inside that space is something we are actively exploring. It is important to us that the central area feels alive, practical and connected to your progression.

I am very glad that the progress tracking stood out to you. Understanding strengths and weaknesses is part of what makes Tae Bo special, and we want to make sure players can see their improvement clearly. And you could not be more right with your last line. Tae Bo has always been about challenge, discipline and a bit of fire in the belly.

Thank you again for such a kind and insightful review. Your feedback helps shape where the experience goes next and we appreciate it more than you know.

reviews.review_description: Excellent presentation, really good menus and tutorials.  Coach is awesome.  So far no subscriptions noticed... Awesome!  My issues so far is my controllers and the end of my knuckles go a few inches past the gloves in the game, not sure if that's intended.  Next thing I find a bit weird is the hook targets are really far off to the side.  It's a good reach but honestly it feels like you are encouraging me to stand square instead of in a normal boxing stance.  I am all for making me work harder for it but by the time I reach my hook all the way over there, especially my rear hook, I'm making contact with the target well past the peak of the punch (where I'd expect my hook to crack normally), so it's not very satisfying to go for them.  I played another fitness boxing game that even has you switch stances but in this game it feels like everyone will end up standing square which is a bit unfortunate.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for such a well structured and thoughtful review. It is great to hear that the presentation, menus, and tutorials made a good first impression and that you are enjoying the coach experience. We also appreciate you noticing the non subscription model, since that is something many players care about.

Your feedback about glove alignment is very useful. The controllers extending a bit beyond the glove visuals is not intentional, and we are already reviewing hitbox calibration to make sure everything lines up more naturally with the physical hand positions. Small details like that can make a big difference in feel and immersion.

Your comments about the hook targets are especially valuable. You are absolutely right that the current distance encourages a more square stance than a traditional boxing guard. The intention was to make players reach wider for an upper body challenge, but you point out a very valid drawback. When contact happens past the natural peak of the hook, the punch never feels as satisfying as it should. We are taking a closer look at target placement and may adjust spacing so hooks feel cleaner, more realistic, and more rewarding, while still giving a good workout.

Thank you again for taking the time to explain your experience so clearly. Feedback like this helps us refine the mechanics and improve technique based gameplay. Really glad you gave TaeBo Reboot a try, and we hope your next sessions feel even better as we continue polishing the game.

reviews.review_description: Taebo  Reboot is a great workout game, it's a great altenative to  LES MILLS BODYCOMBAT. I was suprise add the new addition of stuff compared to  LES MILLS BODYCOMBAT. eg. having to jump, powerup, and rotation. The books system is a good way to categorize each challenge difficulty! I like variety of levels, different scenery/ music make it a lot more enjoyable  and make it feel less the same. I am highly recommending this fitness/workout game!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your review! We’re really happy you enjoy the variety of levels, music, and scenery, and that the Books system makes the progression clear and motivating. The jumps, powerups, and rotations were designed to bring something fresh compared to other fitness titles, so it’s great to hear you noticed and enjoyed them. We appreciate your recommendation and we’ll keep working hard to make every session even more fun and effective! 🔥

reviews.review_description: This was definitely an interesting experience in which I nearly hit my rooms walls several times. I tried a bunch of the levels, but unfortunately for me, it is not the type of fitness experience that I usually play. There is a lot of potential for this experience to grow and improve. Other then that, I am still giving the experience 4 stars to keep the rating up.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for giving TaeBo Reboot a try and for leaving such an honest review. It means a lot that even though the style was not exactly your usual type of fitness experience, you still explored several levels and took the time to share your thoughts.

I hear you about nearly hitting your walls. Movement intensity varies a lot between levels, and we are already improving center calibration and warning systems to help players stay safely within their play area. Your comment reinforces how important that is.

Even if the core gameplay did not fully match what you normally enjoy, I really appreciate you recognizing the potential for growth. We are actively improving the experience week by week, and player impressions like yours help guide the evolution of the app.

And I want to sincerely thank you for the four star rating. Supporting the project even when it was not your perfect fit is incredibly generous and helps the small team behind the game more than you know.

Wishing you great workouts ahead, whatever apps you choose to use. Thank you again for your time and kindness.

reviews.review_title: It's a good game, but not for beginners.
reviews.review_description: I was lucky enough to play this game before it was released, so this review may change later with updates, etc. The game works great and is really what they say it is. You get a real workout even if you only play for 5 minutes. However, the two-color gloves can sometimes be confusing and difficult for beginners because you really use all parts of your VR that you are less familiar with as a beginner. If you are not a beginner, the game is also challenging for Everyone can dodge, punch, and jump, and you have to do it in real life without controllers. So if you don't like exercising in real life or find the gym boring, this is the perfect game for you.

developer_response.body: Thanks for sharing your experience! You’re right, it’s definitely a real workout, even in just a few minutes. We hear you on the colored gloves and the beginner learning curve, and we’ll keep working on ways to make it clearer and smoother. Hopefully with a bit more polish, we’ll earn that 5-star from you next time 🙂

reviews.review_description:
As someone who's been using Supernatural and Pistol Whip for fitness, and has somewhat tired of Synth Riders and Beat Saber over time, I never thought I'd add another app to my workout regime, but I am absolutely hooked on Taebo Reboot! I legitimately can't stop playing till I'm drenched in sweat! It's been a *long* time since I've done a 50+ minute workout, but that's not the case with Taebo Fitness.

To start off, one of the things that really makes the game stand out is the sheer diversity of moves - even moves I've never seen in other apps I've played. I've not had a VR game get me to jump before, which is a novel touch for fitness. I've played for hours hours, and there are still "moves" that I've not encountered yet as I work through the campaign. I believe I saw kicks in there! The game has many fun, inventive ways of getting you to move/use your full body.

This is a game that's great for people who need a sense of progression to help pull them through and motivate them to get working out. The game is structured into several "books", (each book is a distinct difficulty level), comprised of a structured journey through well over a dozen stages (for each book), introducing new mechanics as it goes. Each book takes place in a unique environment (bambo forest, tropical beach, etc). The medium difficulty book is a fantastic workout for me, which is where I've spent most of my time - the easier difficulties are quite accessible to newcomers, too (I haven't gotten to try the hardest difficulties yet).

In addition to the journey of progression through the "books" (ex: Book of Air, Book of Water, etc), you also earn XP to build up to earning martial arts "belts". I think this sort of gamification is great to motivate people to workout, especially newcomers and those who generally struggle with motivation.

The only real negative I've seen is a glitch where the audio/vocal cue telling me to turn to face a new direction was delayed, and arrived *after* the targets already started flying from that new direction - but that was a pre-release glitch (I'm playtesting this game), and I reported the glitch, so I'm certain the devs will fix it in a timely manor. I suppose some would find the lack of licensed music (pop, rock, etc) to be a negative, but even as someone who's super picky about choosing workouts in Supernatural with only music I know and like to listen to, the lack of it didn't bother me at all in Taebo Reboot. I was still just as sucked in.

As I noted above, I AM playtesting this game, so I got my copy for free. But I'm rather happy to recommend Taebo Reboot to paid players - the devs have built a fantastic workout app with solid vibes! It's also *not* a subscription model, and I really value one-time purchase workout apps, as I'm sure most other users do as well.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your incredible and detailed review. We’re thrilled to hear that Taebo Reboot has become part of your fitness routine and that it managed to stand out even among titles like Supernatural, Pistol Whip, and Beat Saber. It’s amazing to know that you’re enjoying the variety of moves, the progression through the Books, and the full-body experience we designed to keep every session challenging and motivating.

You’re absolutely right about the sense of progression being at the heart of the game. Our goal was to make every workout feel like a journey rather than just a repetition of exercises, with each environment offering a new level of focus and energy.

Thank you also for reporting the audio timing glitch during playtesting. We’re already on it, and your feedback helps us polish the experience for everyone.

We truly appreciate your recommendation and support. It means a lot coming from someone experienced in VR fitness. Thanks for sweating it out with us and helping shape Taebo Reboot into the best workout experience it can be. 💪

reviews.review_description:
10/05/25 This VR Fitness Game offers short intense workout , while its growing alongside your progress. It is boxing based and different playful elements keeps your mind sharp and engaged. I believe that it’s suitable for fitness enthusiasts as well as beginners on their fitness journey, offering an impressive amount of available training sessions. Different to other fitness games this one values visualizing training stats highly, e.g. target precision for left and right hands separately. At this point of time I’ve only completed the first book. Therefore my review is based on that experience. 

There is the base game of 7 “books” differring in intensity with around 20 locked levels and bringing a new training environment each. Additionally to the main game are 3 mini games available.
I really love throughout, but useful tutorials. Here, they are presented in form of short videos that explain movements and are in part complimented by an animation of the appearance it takes on in the next workout level. This can help a lot, learning the right body movements from the start. 

Unique to other Fitness games I own is the variety of movement meachanics integrated into one workout session paired with surprise elements equals to quick thinking and fun chaos. Intensity is raising when in short sequencing, the player has to evade, duck, lean or jump and grab a shield before a one-hit bomb can immediately end the short session. There are even interesting hard-to-predict elements of side-to-side moving targets, time slower and direction changer, forcing max concentration.

On the downside, graphics have some room to grow and the levels can only be unlocked when the previous level is successfully passed. This requires a steep learning curve, making early progress tough, especially for beginners, just to have a broader selection of training options.

On a positive note, I previously ran into some issues with the game not registering inputs correctly whatever I tried on my device, but the developer was quick to respond and actively engaged, offering support instead of going silent - I really appreciate that. (My old post about that can be found below.)

The early access version shows a lot of potential for future development into a truly unique fitness experience and I'm excited to see what's next.

Edit: Found out there is a way to unlock the levels in the settings. 


OLD REVIEW
10/03/25 I just bought the game and can not get passed the select profile screen. The very first thing you have to do is creating a user profile, you are promted to input a user name but NO keyboard is popping up and there is no way to get passed this point. It's a shame, I wishlisted this game before release and am disappointed such a major bug was not caught while testing.

developer_response.body:
Updated response : 

Thank you so much for taking the time to write such a detailed and constructive review. We’re really happy to hear that you’re enjoying the gameplay structure, the tutorials, and the variety of movement mechanics. It means a lot to us that you noticed how we focus on proper form, visualized stats, and keeping both body and mind engaged.

We’re also glad the previous issue you had was resolved and that our support helped. Regarding your feedback, we’re continuously improving graphics and progression flow. In the next update coming very soon, players will be able to replace specific moves or targets with others, making the experience more accessible and flexible for every skill level.

Thank you again for your thoughtful words and encouragement. It’s amazing players like you who help shape Taebo Reboot into the best VR fitness experience possible. 💪

------------------------------------------------------------------
Previous Response:

Thanks for your feedback and sorry for the confusion!
On the profile creation screen, you need to click directly on the empty bar where the username goes, this will make the virtual keyboard appear and let you type your name. Hoping this will fix the issue so you can enjoy the game and maybe revisit your rating once it works smoothly for you.

reviews.review_description: I love this app!! I am a 2nd degree black belt in TaeKwonDo and this is the exact type of workout routine that we do in the studio. All workout scenarios and exercises are clearly explained, the degree (intensity) of the workout varies, and the workout environment is really cool. Easy to navigate, easy on the eyes and ears, and is very motivating. Highly recommend this one.

developer_response.body:
That means a lot coming from someone with real martial arts experience. Thank you for sharing such motivating feedback.

We’re thrilled that the routines and structure reminded you of studio-style training. Authenticity has been one of our main goals from the start, and it’s great to hear it connects with practitioners like you.

We’ll keep expanding the variety and intensity options to make sure both beginners and advanced users find new challenges that fit their level. Your recommendation truly inspires us to keep pushing the experience forward. Thanks again for being part of the Taebo Reboot journey. 🥋

reviews.review_description: VR and workout games go hand in hand. It's a great way to incentivize people who have a hard time getting into the workout lifestyle. And Teabo Reboot is such a fun way to get into that lifestyle. Billy Blanks is back mentoring you and showing each new technique. Once you get the rhythm down, Teabo Reboot helps you keep that rhythm. Make it an every day part of your life and you'll see a drastic improvement on your mindset and your body!

developer_response.body: Wow, what an awesome review! Thank you for sharing that, you really captured what Taebo Reboot is all about. It’s amazing to hear how you’re using it to build consistency and make fitness part of your routine. Billy would be proud to know he’s still inspiring people to move and stay motivated!

reviews.review_description:
Taebo Reboot is a rhythm-based VR boxing and fitness game inspired by Billy Blanks’ classic Taebo workouts. Unlike Beat Saber, where you slice blocks to music, Taebo Reboot focuses on (very basic) real martial arts techniques like punching, dodging, jumping, and weaving in sync with rhythmic cues. It blends workout intensity with game-like progression through themed “books” of levels, each introducing new moves and challenges. The inclusion of video tutorials by Billy Blanks makes it feel more like guided fitness training than just a rhythm game.
The game calls the levels “books”. Each “book” feels like a new chapter in your fitness journey, with themed environments and steadily increasing difficulty.
.
The good stuff:
-	The tutorials are great Billy actually teaches you proper form.
-	Punching feels awesome, targets react to your hits.
-	You unlock “Books” (Earth, Air, Water, etc.), each with new challenges.
-	Book of Water = perfect balance of speed and fun. The dodging felt right, not annoying.
-	You actually get a real workout, you’ll sweat.
Where it drags:
-	The music doesn’t change within each book. 10+ levels, same song. It feels repetitive.
-	That slow-motion mechanic? Super fun, but barely used! Please, more of that.
-	The side-to-side walls (especially in Book of Air) are inconsistent — sometimes you dodge perfectly and still get hit.
-	Combo Master and Dummy in the gym mode don’t register hits well. Combo Master barely reacts;
.
Despite those rough edges, Taebo Reboot still stands out as a fun, sweat-inducing, and structured workout. It captures the energy of real Taebo while gamifying it just enough to keep you motivated. With a little more variety in music, a smoother difficulty ramp after Book of Air, and a few hit detection tweaks, it could easily become one of the best VR fitness experiences out there.

developer_response.body:
Hey Marios3! Thanks a lot for the detailed feedback, really appreciate the time you took to share it.

Just to clarify one point: each challenge in Taebo Reboot actually has its own unique song. You might not have noticed it because the tracks within a single “Book” share the same theme and musical style, which gives them a cohesive feel, but every level does have a different song attached to it.

We’ll definitely take note of your other comments though, especially about the slow-motion mechanic and the wall hit detection. Great insights that help us make the experience smoother and more fun for everyone. Thanks again !

reviews.review_description:
This is so fun and super intense! I love how you learn the necessary skills in a musical way. I think you'll be able to transition these skills to real-life training once you know the basics. There are training sessions where he teaches you how to move, perform body movements, and even how to punch correctly.

When you start from the beginning, it begins at the easiest level. As you progress to the next book, it gets more difficult, which is very good. You are constantly learning, including how to evade any type of attack. Your skills will progress as you move up to more difficult levels.

This is an awesome game, and it has become part of my daily workout. I highly recommend it.

developer_response.body: That’s an awesome message, thank you! Really glad to hear you’re having fun with it and feeling the challenge build as you go. The idea was exactly that: start easy, learn the moves, and keep getting better while having a blast. Love that it’s become part of your daily routine.

reviews.review_title: Intense, Sweaty and a Great Workout
reviews.review_description:
TaeboReboot is an excellent VR boxing workout game for staying fit and it's definitely gonna sweat you. So, expect lots of punches, jumps, and squats that really get your heart pumping. The level system and different “books” based on difficulty make progression satisfying, and the training room is great for practice sessions.

Compared to LesMills Bodycombat VR, there's a lot of room for visuals improvements, another issue is that foot placement feels slightly off when holding side positions, which breaks immersion a bit. Hoping to see MR (mixed reality) support in future updates that would take it to the next level.

Overall, a fantastic workout experience that keeps you coming back!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the solid feedback! Stoked to hear you’re getting a great workout and enjoying the progression. We know there’s room to polish visuals and foot placement, and MR support is definitely on our radar. Really appreciate you sweating it out with us and sharing your thoughts!

reviews.review_description:
Taebo Reboot is in my opinion one of the best Fitness Games on Meta Quest - The Achiement system is very motivating, I felt the urge to try a level several times to get better next time. The training effect is pretty good, my muscles felt sore several times now :D 

I won the game on the site "Giveaway & Videos" fromJérome - you should check that one out aswell

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for sharing your thoughts. It really means a lot to us to hear that TaeBo Reboot has earned a spot among your favorite fitness experiences on the Quest platform. That kind of feedback keeps our whole team fired up.

I am especially happy that the achievement system clicked with you. We put a lot of care into making it feel rewarding without being overwhelming. Hearing that it pushed you to retry levels and chase improvement is exactly the kind of reaction we were hoping for. And sore muscles are the best badge of honor, so I am glad the training effect is hitting the right spot for you.

It is also really cool that you discovered the game through Jérome’s Giveaway and Videos page. He puts a lot of passion into his content and we love seeing players connect with the game through his work.

Thank you again for taking the time to leave such a kind review. Enjoy your sessions and keep crushing those achievements. You are part of what makes this community awesome.

reviews.review_description:
I would give Taebo Reboot 4.5 stars, if it were possible. 

I use my Quest 3 for cardio fitness regularly. Up until this point, Les Mills BodyCombat was the one fitness app that I would hold in a tier above the rest. Now, Taebo Reboot joins that top tier. Taebo Reboot shares many of the great features of BodyCombat: many similar strikes and squats, multiple difficulties with scoring to track progress, and AR functionality. 

I really like the video tutorials included in this app. Not only do you get to watch Billy Blanks describe the strikes and exercises and demonstrate how to perform them correctly, the game also shows you how they translate in-game. These were standouts for me. 

Elsewhere, the music is fine, although it doesn't reach the highs found in BodyCombat. The graphics are good and get the job done. I also credit the developer's attempts to gamify the workouts (however, I could do without the bomb mechanic or at least a rework of it). 

Overall, I would recommend this to those who enjoy BodyCombat and want more of that quality. I would also recommend this to those who are looking to first get into VR fitness apps, especially if you already have a fondness associated with Taebo.

developer_response.body: Thanks for such a thoughtful breakdown. Hearing Taebo Reboot land in that same top tier as BodyCombat means a lot. Glad the tutorials with Billy stood out, we put a lot into making them clear and useful. Noted on the music and the bomb mechanic too, that kind of feedback helps us refine things further. Appreciate the recommendation

reviews.review_description:
This game is a very unique exercise game in the fact that it focuses on teaching you skills then you applying them rather than just throwing you into a rhythm level. You start by building foundations which will help you play the game correctly then you have levels of varying difficulties and types available whenever you want so you can jump around or focus on specific things. I believe the tutorial part and the self paced ease into intensity is where this game shines, for the foundations it feels like you have professionally made training videos to help you establish good basics and then you are thrown into low-medium intensity levels to try out what you have and learned, the game does not hold your hand and when you progress you know you could do it again and have improved from where you previously were so you can tackle more intense levels. The stages are intense and fun in their own aspects and the music generally matches up with what you will be doing physically, the intensity is at your own pace but the more intense levels do not hold back and give the game a more challenging quality. If you are to progress linearly each level feels like a step up from the next, usually not entirely but only in some aspects so the overall progression is done really well. Overall I would suggest this to anyone looking for an exercise game but truly wants to challenge themselves but also has the time and willingness to improve because you cannot do this if you do not focus 
 on the experience and it is by no means a fully relaxed experience.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this amazing and thoughtful review! We’re really happy that you appreciated the structure and philosophy behind Taebo Reboot, starting with proper technique and gradually progressing through intensity. It’s awesome to hear that you noticed and valued that balance between learning, challenge, and fun.

Your comment perfectly captures what makes this experience special, training both the body and the mind while enjoying a true sense of progress. Thanks again for taking the time to share your impressions and for being part of the Taebo Reboot community! 💪🔥

reviews.review_title: Fun workout experience with some casualt
reviews.review_description:
It's a great work out game that brings back taeboo (that is suggested in the name) with Billy Banks that will help you by teaching you how to do jabs,hooks and uppercuts and more in the tutorial that takes about 30 minutes ,and you may think that it will just be the normal Billy banks helping you but as soon as you get in the game there will be an ai version of him speaking which put me off a bit but atleast there is a setting to turn off.

I wish the tutorial was while you were in the level to do the punching right after he explains it to you and I have a complaint about the punching equipment liek the punching bag feels to weak when you punch and so is the same with the combo master.

But other than that the game is a great experience for just the levels having unique level designs and halfway through each book they change having the same vibe but either some where unique or just facing the other direction of the level when you start.

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for your detailed feedback!
We’re really glad you enjoyed the workout and the unique level designs. You’re absolutely right, the tutorial and equipment feedback are areas we’re already improving. In the next update, you’ll be able to train directly within the level while learning moves, and we’re enhancing the impact feeling on bags and the Combo Master to make punches feel more powerful and satisfying.

Appreciate your time sharing this, every comment helps us make Taebo Reboot better! 💪

reviews.review_description:
Follow the music and hit those targets., jump over obstacles, duck for walls, and avoid bombs. That’s it.
The difficulties are classified in books from easy to hard. 
Rounds aren’t that long.

Music is very good. And even when you are in the lobby…. Great music. 

Collecting trophies and belts, makes it more challenging.

I only can recommend TAEBO REBOOT.

developer_response.body: Glad you’re having fun with the music and the challenges. The trophies and belts were made to keep that extra push going, thanks for recommending it!

reviews.review_description: As a TaeBo enthusiast from back in the VCR days,  I wasn't sure what to expect from this VR incarnation.  I was very pleasantly surprised.  Billy Blanks is the narrator throughout the tutorial,  which is top notch for learning the basics.  It's broken into different chapters named after elements. I love the workout environment, and you are able to play fully immersed in VR, or you can switch to MR so you can see your room with elements of the game imposed throughout.  What I love most about this fitness app is that unlike other boxing apps that focus entirely on jabs and hooks, this app also focuses on ducks, jumps, and avoiding red walls as they approach!!  Since this is an early release,  there are a few minor items that can be tweaked. For example, a few of my punches did not register, like my glove went right through the target but was counted as a miss. That being said,  that happens in all boxing games. Also, my center somehow moved and was really close to my TV, which, you guessed it,  I ended up hitting my TV screen.     I love that each set shows you a timer, so you know how much time you have left per set.... which is awesome when you're exhausted and may want to quit.... if you see you only have a minute, you'll push through to complete it.  Some of the targets move,  so you have to catch their cadence in order to hit them.  All in all,  even though I wasn't working out for long,  I was huffing and puffing and sweating.  I'm looking forward to seeing how this app develops. I'm hoping they incorporate kicks, if it's safe to do so...

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this amazing review. Hearing from someone who trained with TaeBo back in the VCR years truly means a lot to us, and it is great to know the spirit of the original workouts still connects with you in VR.

I am really glad you enjoyed Billy’s presence in the tutorial. We worked closely with him to make sure the guidance feels authentic, encouraging, and true to the classic style. The element based chapters were designed to create a sense of progression and immersion, so it is great to hear that this resonated with you too. Your comments about the environments and the ability to switch between full VR and MR also mean a lot because comfort and choice are big priorities for us.

Your appreciation for the movement variety really stands out. Ducking, jumping, and navigating the red walls are core to bringing TaeBo back to its roots. It is fantastic to know this part of the workout felt fun and engaging for you.

You also pointed out a few technical issues, and I really appreciate you mentioning them in such a constructive way. We are improving hit detection and smoothing out edge cases where a glove might pass through a target. We are also refining center point detection to help prevent situations like drifting toward a TV. I am sorry your screen took a hit, and we are taking this type of feedback seriously as we continue to polish the experience.

The timer motivation trick is something we are thrilled you noticed. That little push at the end of a set can make a huge difference in effort and satisfaction. And of course the moving targets and cadence work are part of keeping each session dynamic and challenging.

Thank you as well for mentioning kicks. Many people are asking for them, and we are studying how to include them safely without risking balance or furniture collisions. If we can do it right, we absolutely want to bring them in.

Really appreciate you taking the time to share your experience and your excitement. It means the world to the whole team, and we are excited to keep improving TaeBo Reboot for dedicated fans like you.

reviews.review_description:
Early Access Review: Taebo Reboot

Full disclosure: I was provided a copy of this game for free to playtest and review.

Bottom Line:  A fitness game worthy of your time. 

Overview: If you were alive during the 90’s, chances are good you’ve heard of Tae Bo, a series of martial arts fitness videos lead by its creator, Billy Blanks. Anyone who ever tried Tae Bo knows why it remains popular; combining dance, boxing, and karate is a potent and engaging routine. 

The wizards at XPR Labs have translated the essence of what made Tae Bo so much fun (and exhausting, as in the fitness can kick your butt) and distilled it into VR for a new generation to enjoy (as well as those that remember, in fact, there are some workout videos on display in the gym which tip a glove to the nostalgia of the time). 

Gameplay is divided into a series of books, each book containing its own play area, as well as difficulty levels and challenges. The first book, Fundamentals, walks you through how to play, with introduction videos from the great Billy Blanks himself. The subsequent books increase in difficulty and change landscapes, each providing its own challenges. 

As someone who enjoys all kinds of VR, including fitness apps/games, I found Tae Bo to be endearing and fun. So much so that even though Early Access has some rough patches (that the developers are hard at work updating), the gameplay and atmosphere keeps me returning for more. 

Areas for growth: To be clear, I enjoy this game and stand by my rating, even with these small issues as I believe they will be addressed. 

- Some of the user interface could benefit from a sleeker, more VR-friendly design (such as using the analog sticks to scroll through text instead of click up/down arrows).  
- The ability to change glove/pad colors (for those that associate blue to our right hand because of other games).
- A way to disable jumps (as they are not safe moves for those new to VR, especially without a footpad to track location).
- Turning off instant-lose conditions (and just replacing them with score penalties, yes there is a god mode that can negate those concerns, but I’d rather see another option, especially for new gamers as losing is an important teacher, you just don’t want it to stop game flow).
- Custom Tracks, hope there is a way to upload our own music and create our own routines (there can still be ways to potentially add income to the game with new environments or more Billy phrases of encouragement as add-ons).
- More Billy Blank Videos and sayings; I love what’s already in the game, like Blue Oyster Cult and cowbell, this game could always use more. 

Areas that rock: 

- The move set, when you get into that flow state, it is a great fitness feeling, this game delivers in that area time and again I end my session a happy (albeit sweaty) fitness warrior. 
- Billy Blanks videos, great to see both the throwbacks to the 90’s, and the new stuff recorded specifically for this game. Also love his words of encouragement in your ear during a workout, great little motivator to push harder and dig deep. 
- The Music, the tunes work well with the routines and get your heart pumping and your body moving.
- The Books, I enjoyed the progression and different challenges each area had to offer and that players with higher aptitudes can jab right to those books. 

I can see what this game is trying to do and I believe that they will succeed, if this was a finished release I’d give it 4-stars, but knowing that more stuff is on the way, and enjoying what is already there (this game has made it into my 2-3 fitness game routine), for early access I’m giving it 5-stars to show that I believe in XPR and in Billy’s commitment to making not just a good game, but a great one.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this incredible and detailed review. We really appreciate how thoroughly you captured the essence of Taebo Reboot and what makes it special. It means a lot that you recognized the mix of nostalgia, structure, and innovation that we aimed to bring into VR while staying true to Billy’s original spirit and energy.

Your thoughtful breakdown of what works and what could grow is incredibly valuable. The feedback about glove color options, jump settings, and smoother UI navigation is already being taken into account, and we are working on ways to make customization and accessibility even better. We also love that you mentioned the flow state and the motivation from Billy’s voice, since those moments are exactly what we hope players experience every session.

It is great to know the game earned a spot in your regular fitness rotation. Thank you again for believing in what we are building and for taking the time to share such an insightful and supportive review.

reviews.review_description: Taebo Reboot is next-level! 🥊🔥 The workouts are super fun, the music keeps you pumped, and the moves make you feel powerful while burning calories. It doesn’t even feel like exercise—it feels like a blast every time I put on the headset. This is hands down my new favorite way to work out in VR!

developer_response.body: Love hearing this!🤩 That’s exactly what we were going for, workouts that feel powerful and fun at the same time. Thanks for making Taebo Reboot part of your routine!💪

reviews.review_description: I played for 20 minutes and my legs hurt for 6 days.... Great workout game

developer_response.body:
Wow, that is quite the reaction. Twenty minutes turning into six days of sore legs is the kind of compliment every VR fitness developer secretly dreams of. It means the intensity hit exactly where it needed to.

I am really glad the workout gave you that kind of punch. TaeBo has always been known for waking up muscles you did not even remember you had, and it sounds like the VR version kept that tradition alive for you.

Thank you for dropping such a simple but powerful review. Hope your next session feels just as effective, maybe with fewer days of limping around after.

reviews.review_description:
Pros: The workout was fun, engaging, and had a good learning curve. There was a lot of content to work through and the game is still being ironed out, so I have hopes that it will continue to improve in many features. The music is good on some levels, nothing to write home about on others.
 
The main reason I enjoyed this game is as a method to build up a light sweat when I want to stay indoors. Its a good option for this, and it can help you improve your boxing form through practice. I do have my doubts when it comes to the efficacy of the lessons, as there seem to be ways to get good stats at the end of every level despite not having great form... but it is still fun imo.

Cons: Some bugs and practice workouts currently don't work fully, but I don't see them as the main content of the game. Also, the dummies sometimes have a very difficult time actually hitting the right spot for some reason. It would be helpful to make the guides for the practice minigames more elaborate.

Will continue to play!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for taking the time to share your honest and thoughtful review. We’re really glad to hear that you enjoyed the workouts, the variety of content, and the way Taebo Reboot helps you stay active indoors while improving your boxing form. It’s great that you found it both engaging and effective for light, consistent training.

You’re absolutely right about the bugs and practice workouts. We’re aware of those issues and are actively improving them. The next updates will include fixes for target precision, more reliable practice systems, and better tutorials for the minigames to make progression smoother and clearer.

We truly appreciate your feedback and support as we continue refining the experience. Thank you for being part of the Taebo Reboot community and for helping us make the game stronger with every update. 💪

reviews.review_description:
This game is nice !
So, it's about boxing and moving a bit, the mecanics are realy interesting :
That forces you to do boxing moves, dodge, and even jump ! That's the first game i've seen with this mecanic !

The difficulty is getting harder on each book but i've seen harder games, i would probably be bored over time.

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for your review. We’re glad you enjoyed the boxing mechanics and the fact that Taebo Reboot gets you to move, dodge, and even jump. It’s great to hear that the gameplay felt fresh to you, especially with that added jump element.

We appreciate your honesty about the difficulty. More challenging content and higher intensity modes are already in development to keep advanced players engaged over time.

Thanks again for giving it a try and for sharing your thoughts.

reviews.review_description: Some of the punches are not that accurate. Im finding I need to do the move exactly as Billy stated in the video or else (which is good I guess) the music is sooooooo boring! I had to find a playlist of spotify with the 90 BPM that matches in order to have good music cause its just not motivating. I got in shape with Billy back in the early 2000's and this was a very welcome reboot as I tried the old videos and they just felt dated obviously lol. I absolutley love the heavy bag workout and I look forward to future updates.

This game has some elements of combat training, minigames you'd find say in a boxing gym but catered to be shorter exercises to give you options besides the usual workout series you'd find in other fitness apps. Those are here too and you'll find a lot of different difficulties and paths to get you from easy to very, very active. That upgrade system will definitely put you on the right track to understand how the app works. It does give you a good cardio workout if you want it to. There are some things I hope they fix with audio and perhaps add more music but if you've tried apps like Les Mill and liked it I do recommend checking this one out and see jf it fits your routine.

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to share your thoughts! Glad to hear you enjoyed the cardio and variety with the workouts and mini-games. We’re already working on fixing the audio issues and adding more features in the next updates. Hopefully with those improvements, we’ll earn that 5 stars from you next time! 😁

reviews.review_title: Game starts off fun but had some flaws
reviews.review_description:
What it is: boxing fitness game.

Gameplay and core loop: complete different books of workouts that each comprise of a various amount of shadowboxing workouts where you punch targets, dodge walls, spikes and bombs and aim to get over the minimum power, accuracy  and completion percentage.

 First book is all about punching fundamentals (or kicking if you have leg trackers). The rest are titled as elemental books (book of earth, fire  etc...) this sounds cool at first glance but you quickly realize the exercises are pretty much all the same, just get a little harder each book. 

It quickly becomes repetitive and loses momentum. Learning the fundamentals was cool, and done well. But after that its like they abandoned the teaching/learning pursuit and tried to make it more of a game. It says it has "boss" fights but really its just another workout. It would've been cool to have an actual enemy you are battling. 

The environments are pretty cool, they look good. I think this is a good start to a great game if they make it more purposeful with completing the books and maybe add better music. I like the idea of the spikes and bombs flying. I like that there are jumps. It is a great exercising tool. But if you're looking for something more captivating and purpose driven, this isnt it. 


Other annoyances: 

Feet in the pad doesnt show stance. I think its important to show that and also it would be fun to have stance changes throughout. 

Its hard to know when and where the targets will come from a different direction. I am not looking at my feet to see the arrow and sometimes I tune out the floating light thing that moves. There needs to be something more obvious like the left or right side of the screen changing colors. 

In the main menu if you move to another location, most of them dont give an option to move back to the menu location and the menu doesnt follow you. 

Overall I think its a cool game and you will definitely get a good workout, but dont assume you are getting a full Tae Bo course or a fully progressive game.  Its only good if you just wanna do a few workouts to get your exercise in.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the detailed review, really appreciate the time you took to share both the positives and the areas to improve. We’re glad you enjoyed the fundamentals and the workout challenge, and we hear you on wanting more variety, progression, and purpose beyond the elemental books.

Your suggestions about stance visibility, clearer directional cues, and a more dynamic menu experience are super valuable, we’re already looking into updates that address exactly these kinds of details. Music and progression are also on our roadmap, since we want the game to stay as engaging as it is effective.

This is just the beginning for Taebo Reboot, and feedback like yours helps us level it up into the complete VR fitness experience people are asking for. Thanks again for giving it a shot and for sharing your thoughts!

reviews.review_description: Seeing this in the store dragged me back to the 90s, and being the nostalgic person I am, figured that within the privacy of my headset I could give this a go in 2025.  On the inside, a little bit worried about how I'd fare....but this is where this app shone the brightest for me.  I've been in a COVID-era slump and I needed something to wake my body back up.  I knew it would have to be fun.  And this app.....is.....fun!  I can't get through most of the books.  Heck on some it's beyond my skill level right now.  But there is a ton I can access at my level for the length of time I need it, with TONS of room for skill development.  And it's not boring. Even if starting out and playing in the fundamentals section, each round is just a couple of minutes.  As you pass through each you learn a new skill and/or the intensity of the mastered skills increases.  The books reinforce all of it.  There's also individual parts of the gym you can workout in if you wanted to.  Also included are videos of Billy Blanks that show/explain the routines.  I liked being able to review the power and accuracy of my punches across each routine.  I had other workout apps on my headset.  I've replaced all of them with this one.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your story and energy with us! It’s awesome to hear how the game helped you shake off that slump and bring back that 90s spark 🔥 We made Taebo Reboot exactly for that, to make moving fun again and help everyone find their rhythm at their own pace. Huge respect for sticking with it and growing through the books, you’re exactly the kind of motivation that keeps us creating more. Keep punching, keep smiling, and welcome back to the Taebo spirit 👊💚

reviews.review_description:
Hope you're ready to work up a sweat.  This is a great fitness game with a rythem style focus.
