You will be punching, jumping, and dodging your way through multiple books with their own unique environments.  Each book consists of 20 levels with increasing difficulty throughout.  While the fundamentals have Billy teaching you proper techniques.  You will get a great fitness routine that also keeps you on your toes needing quick reaction speeds.  

There is personal leader boards for you and other local accounts with online leader board coming later down the road.    These mainly focus on the mini games you can do practice.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the detailed review! Awesome to hear you’re diving into the Books and pushing through the levels, that’s exactly how it’s meant to be played. We’re really glad you’re enjoying the rhythm and challenge mix, and yep, those leaderboards are just the beginning.

reviews.review_description: There's a flood of fitness games out there so I like to see this genre of game bring something new to the plate.  That's exactly what this game did to me.  It had many of the familiar moves of punches and jabs and swaying back and forth but it also adds actual jumping which increases the challenge and makes my heart beat even faster and harder.  I love that it brings many different relaxing backgrounds which helps take me away from the stresses of my real world.  I love that it has money that you grab that you can collect. Makes me feel I am earning something along with the exercise component.  It helps with the motivation.  I would love to see new dlcs with popular music selections in the future.  The music already in it is great for the game already though.

developer_response.body:
Really appreciate your awesome review. It is great to hear that Taebo Reboot stood out for you among other fitness games. The fact that you enjoyed the challenge of jumping, the variety of environments, and the feeling of earning rewards means a lot to us.

We designed the game to keep both your body and mind engaged, so knowing that it helped you disconnect from daily stress while staying motivated makes us really proud.

Your suggestion about adding popular music in future updates is noted, and we are glad you already enjoy the current soundtrack. Thanks again for your feedback and for being part of the Taebo Reboot community.

reviews.review_description:
Its pretty good when looking for a small workout. Its basically a rythm game with more focus on getting to sweat, but it also includes some other mini games to switch it up a bit. The movements you have to do seem to be similar to the fitness videos from billy blanks and they do feel effective, atleast according to my sore body the next day :)
I also appreciate that theres some instructions included on like correct postures.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your review! We’re really happy that you’re enjoying Taebo Reboot and that it gave you a solid workout. It’s great to hear that you appreciate the blend of rhythm and fitness mechanics, as well as the guidance on proper form. That soreness the next day is proof that you put in some real work!

We’re continuing to refine the mini games and expand the training content so you’ll always have new ways to stay active and motivated. Thanks again for sharing your experience and for being part of the Taebo Reboot community! 💪

reviews.review_description:
Prepare a towel before you play this. I assure you will sweat buckets even after 15 min of playing. This is definitely my go to workout app for me.

The punching is fun, the stat/score after each session is great. If there is a con, the pad sometimes cover the one behind it. Example in fast straight and hook/uppercut pattern, the hook pad cannot be seen at all so you have little time to react. Another concern i have are jumps. My worry is my headset might slip from my head jump and fall.

If there is an input u can give, is hopefully there will be AI model/bot in the room doing whatever movement/game perfectly as an example to motivate me and make me less lonely.

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for sharing your thoughts. We’re really happy to hear that Taebo Reboot has become your go-to workout app and that it keeps you sweating and motivated. It’s awesome that you’re enjoying the punching mechanics and the stats after each session.

You’re right about the pad visibility and the jumps. We’re already looking at ways to improve pad placement in fast combos and to make jumps feel safer for players worried about their headset. We also love your idea of having an AI training partner or model in the room showing the moves perfectly to keep you motivated, that’s exactly the kind of feedback that helps us plan future features.

Thanks again for playing and for taking the time to share your experience.

reviews.review_description: So far i enjoyed all different levels ans it difficulty. I havnt came across to much issues accept a few lag spikes or small freeze ups.  But overall it gets me active and I recommend to all friends who wanna stay fit and active. i look forward to seeing more levels in the future

developer_response.body: Thanks a ton for the awesome feedback! Really glad to hear you’re enjoying the levels and getting a solid workout from it. We’re constantly working on optimizing performance, so those little lag spikes should smooth out in upcoming updates. Appreciate you recommending it

reviews.review_title: What a great way to work out
reviews.review_description: I loved playing this game. This game is slow enough for beginners, will teach you everything you need to know step by step with expert coaching and is an app that doesn't want to see you fail but learn form your mistakes and keep going and pushing yourself. I felt at ease and totally comfortable with everything this game presented and thought it did so in a way to get the best workout possible. I kept going back to this app to improve myself, better my training and techniques, and left this app always feeling extremly good about myself and the workout I achieved. Would recommend this app to everyone. It is and you will have a great experience with it.

developer_response.body:
Reading your message truly made our day. Thank you for taking the time to share such encouraging words and for capturing the spirit of what Taebo Reboot is all about.

Our goal has always been to create a space where anyone can train, grow, and feel proud of their progress. Knowing that you felt supported, motivated, and confident while playing means everything to us.

We’ll keep building on that foundation with new content, more coaching depth, and ways to help everyone stay consistent on their journey. Thanks again for recommending it and for being part of the Taebo Reboot community. Keep pushing forward, and stay strong!

reviews.review_description: I’ve tried several workout games on the quest and this is definitely my favorite one. The fact that you start out by learning the fundamentals and are able to go back and redo them as needed is great, as opposed to the usual quick tutorial. The inclusion of various training areas that almost feel like a home gym if you don’t feel like doing a dedicated workout session and just want to do your own thing, and then we have the actual “books” allowing you to progress through harder and harder workouts. If you’re a beginner there’s something for you, if you’re proficient, there’s something for you, this game will keep you moving and my personal favorite part? A one time payment and not an annoying subscription!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your awesome review. We are thrilled to hear that Taebo Reboot has become your new favorite workout game. It is great that you appreciated the focus on fundamentals and the flexibility to train freely in the gym or progress through the Books at your own pace.

We wanted the experience to feel like a complete training journey, whether you are just starting out or already experienced, and your feedback shows that it is hitting that mark. And yes, we are with you on the one time purchase, no subscriptions, just a full fitness experience you can enjoy anytime.

Thanks again for your support and for being part of the Taebo Reboot community.

reviews.review_description: I received the game for free in return for testing.  I haven't played other workout games because I didn't like the idea of sweating in my headset, but I decided to try it to start improving my health.  This is one game that I have continued to play on a daily basis even after my testing had completed, which is an indicator of how much I have liked the game even though it is not the type of game I would normally consider.  I found that Taebo Reboot presents a very easy on-ramp to slowly increase the level of exercise I do.  I can play anywhere from 5 minutes to as long as my battery lasts, but often I just jump in for 15 minutes after dinner.  You can start out with low effort but the game encourages you to put in more effort as you continue with feedback on how you are punching and periodic achievements.  On the surface the game is simple but very polished, but when you are ready for it you can dig into statistics on how you are doing left vs right, punch speed, etc.  I like how the game is split between "fundamentals" that teach and allow you to practice different techniques and levels of increasing difficulty.  All-in-all it has been a very positive experience for me and I have never felt a desire to finish it and move on to something else.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for your thoughtful review. It is great to hear that Taebo Reboot helped you get into VR fitness comfortably and that it has become part of your daily routine. The fact that you kept playing even after testing means a lot to us.

We are really glad you enjoyed the smooth progression from fundamentals to more challenging levels, as well as the feedback and stats that help you see your improvement. The goal was exactly that, to make fitness accessible, motivating, and rewarding whether you want a quick five minute session or a full workout.

Your feedback perfectly captures what we hoped to achieve, and we are happy it has had such a positive impact on your experience. 💪

reviews.review_title: Best VR Workout App I've tried to date.
reviews.review_description: From the first time I started playing, this game makes you sweat. Very comfortable viewing experience, yet still a vigorous exercise. Every time I played, I couldn't help but hop back on as soon as I could catch my breath. Extremely enjoyed the challenges and found myself wanting to continue instead of getting bored and putting it down. This engaged every part of my body. This is a must own for any fitness enthusiast or anyone wanting a full body workout. Love this game, would give 10* if available.

developer_response.body: Wowww thank you so much for this amazing review! 🙌 We’re so happy to hear that Taebo Reboot gave you that full-body sweat and kept you hooked instead of bored. The fact that you’d give it 10 stars means the world to us. We’ll keep adding more challenges and content so you always have reasons to hop back in. Thanks for being part of the journey! 💪

reviews.review_title: The most fun I've had in a workout game
reviews.review_description:
Right off the bat: This experience delivers what it promises on the tin. This is a gamefied workout game. 

So, confession: I went into Taebo Reboot thinking it’d be a kitschy throwback. Like, “oh cool, VR Billy Blanks yelling at me to do squats in a cosmic dojo.” Instead, ten minutes later I was sweating buckets, throwing my whole body into side kicks, and muttering, “okay Billy, I get it, you’re right, my form sucks.”

This isn’t just a novelty fitness app. It’s an honest-to-goodness workout. And it’s… kind of awesome.

If you grew up in the 90s or 2000s, Tae Bo was everywhere. Your mom’s VHS shelf, late-night infomercials, gyms. Here, Billy himself is your coach again, voice guiding you through punches, kicks, and dodges. It’s both motivating and slightly terrifying, in the best way. You will move, because Billy said so.

The game splits into five “Books” themed around Earth, Water, Fire, Air, and Emptiness (which sounds like my fridge at 2 a.m.). Each one ramps up difficulty with over a hundred challenges.

The cool part? You’re not just standing in one spot. You’re weaving, ducking, side-stepping, sometimes even leaping over walls. It’s like Beat Saber’s aggro cousin who did martial arts in college and still talks about it.

Every workout logs your precision, power, and agility. You can unlock belts and gloves, which scratches that “gamer brain wants rewards” itch. It’s surprisingly satisfying seeing your form improve—or at least watching the numbers trick your brain into believing you’re a ninja.

I had a lot of fun with this one, and if you are looking for a gamefied workout, this one has ya covered.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for this amazing and detailed review! We love how you captured the spirit of Taebo Reboot, it really is about turning workouts into something fun, challenging, and rewarding. Hearing that you were sweating buckets, moving with kicks and dodges, and feeling that Billy energy in VR is exactly what we hoped players would experience. We are glad you enjoyed the Books system, the rewards, and the variety of movement. Your words motivate us to keep making the game better and better. Thanks again for recommending it and for jumping in with such enthusiasm!

reviews.review_description:
(product received for free evaluation)
Taebo-Reboot is a game that will make you move, sweat, swear, dodge, duck, dip, dive, and dodge. It is an experience that even as a fitness game expert will take you a long time to truly master, and for the novice has Billy there showing you in detail how to take your first steps down every path.

The core mechanics are simple: Various punches, dodges, jumps, and squats. The mix of those elements along with speed and choreographed complexity continue to step up as you proceed through "books" of elements. Each book brings you to a different environment as a background to your workout and each chapter gives you different challenges to complete to master. You score both points for competing against yourself and your friends, and coins for buying in game items.

Gameplay was well done for tracking and hit registering in both speed and power.  I really enjoyed the "training" tools like heavy bag and the body opponent bag (BOB). They felt well thought out and implemented.

Pro/Con list:
Pros
   Game elements to keep workout "fun" (IF this interests you)
   No monthly fee
Cons
   Game elements distract from fitness (IF this is unwanted)
   Little to draw you back to earlier workouts (once mastered)

Fitness is a very personal experience as what works for one person is not for everyone. IF you are the kind of person who popped in the original Taebo VHS or worked to P90x DVDs, and need some game elements to keep you challenged, then this could be the application for you. It feels much like a Lee Mills Body Combat that added additional game mechanics (slow time, bombs, shields).
If you are more like a Peloton user or a Supernatural subscriber, you may want to watch some gameplay to make sure you understand this is more game-like. You can and will "fail" a workout causing it to end. There are not billboard artist songs accompanying workout to have you going back to a simple yet favorite one for warmups or cooldowns. 

A solid fitness GAME

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your detailed and balanced review. We really appreciate how thoughtfully you described your experience with Taebo Reboot. It is great to hear that you enjoyed the mix of punches, dodges, and jumps, and that the training tools like the heavy bag and BOB felt well designed and satisfying to use.

You captured the essence of what we aimed for, a true fitness game that blends skill progression with physical challenge. Your notes on the game-like elements and their impact on different types of players are spot on, and we value that perspective a lot.

We are glad the core mechanics and tracking felt solid, and we will keep improving features to make each workout more rewarding and customizable for every kind of player. Thanks again for your honest feedback and for helping others understand what Taebo Reboot brings to the table.

reviews.review_description:
The number of different things you can do is AMAZING! The different environments, books, and mini-games all come together to make an amazing game. I would 100% recommend Taebo to anyone looking for a new, modern, and very fun workout game.

Note: Taebo Reboot was provided for free to complete playtesting.

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for your great review. We’re really happy to hear that you enjoyed the variety of environments, books, and mini games, and that the whole experience came together for you. It means a lot that you would recommend Taebo Reboot to others looking for a fun and modern workout game.

We appreciate you taking the time to playtest and share your thoughts. Your feedback helps us keep improving and delivering the best possible experience for every player. 💪

reviews.review_title: A serious workout that’s also fun
reviews.review_description:
A rhythms-style boxing game I thought would be like all the others. 

Starts out with jabs, crosses, upper cuts, dodging side to side or down. But then they just keeps adding so much more.  
Jumping that actually works very well! Dodging spikes that can take off health energy. Bombs that can take you out in one hit. Collect shields to allow you to take more damage or survive a bomb.  Collect hourglasses to slow down time, allowing you to survive crazy parts of the level.  And even levels where the targets change the direction of where they come at you. 
A lot of content to start with. A fundamental book and a variety of other elemental books with many workouts inside. A couple of mini-games that also give a great workout and a lot of fun.

I’m a fan of Billy Blanks and I like the whole layout and set up. I like his videos that walk you through the movements and positions of the gameplay. I like the retro style layout and background with videos playing or movie posters. 

The only negative criticism that I can find so far is that some levels seem random.  Where others have a smooth, purposeful flow to them.  The same problem as other rhythm games or workout games. So not a big deal and still great fun

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for your awesome and detailed review. We are really happy that you enjoyed how Taebo Reboot builds layer after layer of gameplay beyond the basics. It is great to hear that the mix of jumping, dodging, shields, hourglasses, and direction changes kept things exciting and challenging for you.

We are glad you liked Billy’s presence, the tutorials, and the retro layout that ties everything together. Your note about level flow is helpful, and we are continuing to refine pacing and rhythm so each stage feels even more cohesive and intentional.

Thanks again for the kind words and for being part of the Taebo Reboot community. It means a lot that you are having fun while getting a serious workout.

reviews.review_description:
Taebo Reboot is a blend of boxing, rhythm, and cardio in one fun package. The immersive environments and on the go coaching make for a highly motivating and effective workout. 

I haven't been able to try the higher difficulties yet but I'm working towards them. This is a must-try VR fitness game, still in early access - there's room for improvement but there's huge potential here!!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your detailed review! We’re glad you’re enjoying the mix of boxing, rhythm, and cardio and that the immersive environments and coaching keep you motivated. The higher difficulties will definitely give you an extra challenge once you get there. Since we’re still in Early Access, we’re working hard on improvements and new content, and your feedback helps us shape the game. Thanks for highlighting the potential and for recommending Taebo Reboot! 💪🔥

reviews.review_description: The game combines workout with games and makes a boring workout session into an exciting game. This is really good for people who like to exercise but aren't that excited about it. It has different levels with different difficulty levels, different environments and also different movements( jumping, dodging and ...). There is a level for everyone whether you are a  beginner or a pro. Overall I really liked and enjoyed playing it and it made me really tired like a real exercise.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the awesome review! Happy to hear it kept things exciting and gave you that “real workout” feeling. We designed the levels so everyone can find their pace, and it’s great to know that came through. Appreciate you playing and sharing your experience!

reviews.review_title: You Will Definitely Work Up A Sweat!
reviews.review_description:
If you want a VR workout app that pushes you to your limits - this is the one to get. There are a wide variety of activities be they workouts or training or tutorials. There are a lot of exercises so you won't blow through the tutorials in 5 minutes. What makes this one unique is not just the variety of targets but how they move. You really need to be on your toes not only to hit the targets but also to dodge, weave, and jump over barriers. All the barriers work well with the exception of the jumps. They are hard to judge properly and may be too much for any with poor knees. An option to turn off the jump barriers would be a welcome addition. 

Get ready for a great workout!

developer_response.body: Right on, this one’s built to push, and it’s great to hear the variety of targets and movement stood out. Totally hear you on the jump barriers, accessibility options like that are on the way. Thanks for sharing your take and for putting in the work

reviews.review_description:
This game is a fun step into the world of Tae Bo. The environments bring a classic  90s workout energy to the vr space. The difficulties are divided into "elements." The fire and emptiness challenges were really tough. The game has you bobbing and weaving a lot, and throwing some crazy punches. I noticed my form getting better every time I played.

The sound design deserves its own section here too. The music is really great and fits the theme perfectly, but you also have Tae Bo legend Billy Blanks giving vocal encouragement during each workout. He says a lot of great stuff to keep you going and it really adds a personal touch to the whole experience.

 I play a lot of vr workout games, and this one is a must own.

developer_response.body: Love how you described it, especially calling out the fire and emptiness challenges. Those are meant to push hard, so it’s awesome you felt your form improving as you went. And yeah, Billy’s voice really does add that extra push. Great to hear this one’s earned a spot in your VR workout lineup

reviews.review_title: Solid Workout Game that has Potential!
reviews.review_description: This game is a great blend of working out and being a video game. There are lots of levels, all with varying difficulties that all skill levels can find a fair amount of challenge in. Variety is definitely a strong suit in this game. You'll be doing various punches, movements, turns, while also being in different environments and soundscapes. Minigames are a great addition however I mainly enjoyed the Book levels. I do wish there was more level of customization, like glove/target colors, height adjustments, and tweaks to accomodate for players who may not be as experienced with boxing, like customizing power and accuracy thresholds, but those can all definitely come into the game later. Very solid release and could easily be a 5 star if it continues development!

developer_response.body: You nailed it. The mix of workouts and game design is exactly what makes it click, and it’s cool to hear you dug the variety of our game. :)

reviews.review_description:
Taebo Reboot is amazing with trainer Billy Blanks! I love how it takes you step by step through all the fundamentals. The app is broken up into 5 books with each book having 20 different challenges and the level of difficulty increases as you progress. All of this is delivered with engaging music to make you move without thinking that you are just exercising. 

Taebo Reboot is for all ages and levels of experience. It teaches you as you progress at your own pace and burns calories at the same time. You get immersed in the rhythm of the punches matching the beat of the music that you forget that you are working out. You'll want to keep coming back to improve your scores and get your exercise done for the day in a rewarding and enjoyable way.

I am 62 years old, I have never done Taebo and have not exercised in quite a while, but this app has gotten me up and moving. I have bad knees but I am still able to do this app. The Developers are looking to add a jump accessibility feature for those that can't jump due to health issues. There is a whole lot more that the Developers will be rolling out in the future for customization. I really like this game and feel that it will be one that I use all the time.

developer_response.body: Reading this put a smile on my face. Seeing you jump back into exercise at 62 and finding it both doable and motivating is exactly the kind of story that keeps us building. We’re working on those accessibility features so everyone can keep moving at their own pace. Thanks for sharing such an inspiring experience.

reviews.review_description:
I remember watching these on VHS tapes but in VR they knocked it out of the park. And this is absolutely a workout but takes you through everything step by step.
With the screens to watch how things are done and challenges or simply hanging in the gym doing free style!
Though this is a true work out at the age of 66, I can still manage to perform these without too much trouble although I will never be at the top! LOL
Really well done and worth a try if you Love work outs!
Whoop, Whoop!

developer_response.body: This is wonderful to read, thank you! We love hearing from longtime Tae Bo fans, and it’s amazing to know you’re enjoying it in VR. Big respect for jumping in and keeping up with the workouts at 66, that’s inspiring. Really glad the step-by-step flow is working for you. Whoop whoop right back at you!

reviews.review_description:
Taebo Reboot is a dynamic VR fitness game combining martial arts, cardio, and rhythmic movements. Supported by Billy Blanks himself and inspired by classic Tae Bo, it fully exploits the potential of VR and MR to make fitness captivating and fun.
The movements are well-calibrated (dodging, body movement, punches) and require speed, strength, and precision.
The interactive coaching measures force (m/s), punch accuracy, and agility via targets.
The environments inspired by natural elements (fire, water, air, etc.) add an immersive dimension that helps you stay focused while remaining highly varied.
You earn rewards and optimize your performance with each session.
The intensity adjusts to your progress, providing you with a personalized challenge.
The interface is clear, without overloading, and energy remains at the heart of the experience.
Ideal for anyone looking to push their level while having fun, halfway between a rhythm game and a demanding fitness program.
A strap that securely holds the Quest on your head is useful during very dynamic phases.
It's an excellent blend of cardio, virtual combat, and gamification for motivation.
You'll come out sweaty... and proud!

developer_response.body: “Powerful review, you can tell you really felt the intensity and flow. That balance of stats, environments, and challenge is what keeps the game alive session after session. We’ll keep pushing it further so it stays a real fitness benchmark.

reviews.review_title: Solid workout with a different spin
reviews.review_description: Received for free for pre-release testing. With the Tae Bo focus, things are a bit different than in other fitness titles. While fundamentally the same as many other games of the type, they try different things like targets that move up and down or side to side to make you concentrate more. Also one of the games that make me sweat, which is why I play these. Movements are explained in short videos, so you know how to do the poses, which is helpful

developer_response.body:
Really appreciate you taking the time to share your impressions and for testing Taebo Reboot early. It means a lot that you noticed the effort to bring something different to the fitness genre.

We wanted to capture the real Tae Bo feeling by mixing traditional moves with dynamic targets that keep you focused and constantly adjusting. It’s awesome to hear it got you sweating and engaged, that’s exactly the goal.

We’re continuing to refine the experience with even smoother target behavior and more workout variety. Thanks again for being part of the pre-release testing and helping us shape the final version.

reviews.review_title: A must have for VR fitness lovers
reviews.review_description:
I was so grateful to be part of the beta test for this game as workout games are my jam and I've played just about all of them. This app brings a critical element that is lacking in most VR fitness apps and makes it a daily go to game for me: structure and linear progression. 

You begin by learning the Fundamentals, then progress through various Books of workouts that increase in difficulty and intensity as you go through them. If you decide to do the program in linear fashion, as you successfully complete each workout, the next one unlocks until the Book is finished. Conversely, you can unlock everything out of the gate and bounce around every element that the game has to offer in any way you choose. This provides structure for those that need it, while allowing others to freely explore and choose their own path.

In addition to the structured program, there is much else to do and more to come! There's a heavy bag, a dummy, and a double ended bag all with built in workouts that you can do outside of the main program. 

The layout of the program is very well done and easy to navigate. The workouts can feel frenetic when there is a lot coming at you, but you're thinking so much about avoiding obstacles and catching others that you won't notice that you're getting in an intense, full body workout. If you'd like to avoid having your workout halted, you can choose God mode and not have to worry about failure. One of the greatest strengths of this game is its versatility.

If you remember Billy Blanks and TaeBo from back in the day, you know it was a serious program. It's no different here, but the added arcade-like elements keep you coming back to tackle some very blistering levels. It's so rewarding to see both progress inside the game along with the health and wellness benefits you get. This hopped to the top of my daily VR fitness routine and will undoubtedly remain there.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this incredible review. We’re really glad you connected with the structure and linear progression of Taebo Reboot, since that balance between guided training and player freedom is exactly what we wanted to bring to VR fitness. It’s great to hear that you’ve made it part of your daily routine and that it stands out even among all the other fitness apps you’ve tried.

We’re also happy that you enjoyed the layout, the variety of training options like the heavy bag and dummy, and the flexibility to switch between structured progression and free play. You perfectly captured the spirit of the game, a serious full body workout powered by Billy’s energy and some fun, arcade style challenges to keep motivation high.

Thank you again for being part of the beta and for such a thoughtful write up. Your support and insight mean a lot to the whole team.

reviews.review_description: I was fortunate enough to play this game early and actually really enjoyed it! It’s a great workout game that has you go through levels punching targets and dodging obstacles to reach the end. The levels start to get really challenging in the later chapters and it was satisfying to perfectly combo through a row of targets. The backgrounds are really nice and make you feel more like a warrior as you go through areas like forests and mountains. The only downside I have is that the game features plenty of jumping which I was struggling with, so keep that in mind if you do buy it. All in all, a very fun fitness game that I had a blast with despite being very uncoordinated and unfit, but I recommend for someone who’s much more active in vr.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the honest review! Glad you had fun with the levels and the different environments, they’re meant to make you feel like you’re really in it. We get that the jumping can be tough, and good news: accessibility options for that are coming in the next updates. Appreciate the recommendation!

reviews.review_description: I had a lot of fun working out in Taebo Reboot. This is somewhat a Beat Saber-like rhythm game, but includes workouts that make you sweat x10 more. One of the things I really enjoyed about this game was the "juice" when hitting the pads in-game. Every time you hit a pad, it would go flying, collide with the wall, and then make the entire room shake, which made me feel powerful and pumped, which motivated me a little. Something that caught me by surprise was every new mechanic you unlock, like the jabs, uppercuts, sidesteps, etc. Instead of explaining the new mechanics via text and pictures, they had an actual person via a short video to walk you through what posture you should have, how you should be using your body, etc. Along with another short video showing gameplay of how that new mechanic would look. Which I found really helpful because when working out, having the correct posture/stance can make working out a lot easier and safer. Quick final thing I liked was seeing a timer on my wrist on how long I've been playing the game, which is nice because it gives me a quick way to time myself and my breaks. What I think the game was lacking was the music while working out, there was one or two songs that really got me pumped and had me swinging my fist with the beat of the song. A lot of the levels didn't really have that. Personally, music keeps me motivated when working out. Something I would like to see in this game is a disclaimer to make sure you stretch or a 15min stretching minigame, because in the first 10 minutes of playing the game, I pulled my shoulder a bit, which hurt lol. But yeah, because it was a game, it didn't occur to me that I was actually working out and that I should have stretched beforehand. Other than that, the game did what it intended to be, which was a workout game. Quick warning, there is a lot of squatting and jumping in this game, your legs have been warned!!!! T-T

developer_response.body: Thanks for the detailed feedback! Glad you enjoyed the power and impact of the pad hits, we really wanted every punch to feel satisfying. Happy to hear the tutorial videos with Billy were useful too. Totally agree on music and stretching: we’re already working on more pump-up tracks. And yes… legs will definitely feel it after all that squatting and jumping! Appreciate you sharing your experience.

reviews.review_description:
We want to take a moment to thank each and every one of you for being part of this incredible journey. The launch of Taebo Reboot has been nothing short of amazing, and it’s because of this community, your encouragement, your feedback, and your passion, that we’ve been able to bring this vision to life.

This game was truly a blast to make. Every punch, every environment, every detail was crafted with the goal of giving you not just a workout, but an experience that connects body, mind, and spirit. And we’re only getting started. We have a ton of new features and updates on the way that will keep pushing the limits of what fitness in VR can be.

Billy Blanks once changed the world by inspiring millions to get moving, to feel stronger, and to believe in themselves. With your help, we can carry that same energy into a new era. Together, we’re not just building a game, we’re building a movement.

Thank you for standing with us. The best is yet to come.

reviews.review_title: A blast of a boxing game
reviews.review_description:
If you’re looking for a fun boxing workout game—or just a workout game in general—this is honestly a great pick. It’s super easy to get into, and the difficulty scales really well as you improve. Whether you’re brand new to boxing workouts or already in decent shape, the game keeps pushing you at just the right pace without feeling overwhelming.

What I really like is that it doesn’t just feel like another rhythm game. The game actually takes into account the strength, accuracy, and timing of your punches, so it feels more like a real workout than just hitting notes on a beat. You’ll notice that the harder and cleaner you throw, the more satisfying it is—both in the game and in your own fitness progress.

And having Billy Blanks guide you through the workouts is such a cool bonus. Over time, you really do start to feel your punches getting sharper and stronger, which makes coming back to the game even more rewarding.

Overall, it’s an awesome mix of fitness and fun. If you want a workout that actually keeps you motivated while also giving you some boxing fundamentals, this is definitely worth checking out.

developer_response.body: Really appreciate this review! You nailed exactly what we were aiming for, we didn’t want just another rhythm workout, but something that feels like real training while still being fun. Awesome to hear the progression and Billy’s guidance are keeping you motivated. Thanks for sharing your experience!

reviews.review_title: One of the Best VR Fitness Games
reviews.review_description:
Taebo Reboot is a fantastic fitness game that perfectly brings Billy Blanks’ iconic program into VR. Tae Bo combines boxing, martial arts, and dance into a total-body workout — the name itself standing for Total Awareness of Excellent Body Obedience.

From the moment you step into the lobby with Billy motivating you in the background, you’re pumped to train. The mix of live-action videos and gameplay works beautifully, and the progression across 9 levels (each with multiple stages) keeps pushing you further at just the right pace.

You’ll hit static and moving pads in all directions, punch high and low, dodge, jump, and keep your body engaged the entire session. The environments are clean, the music is energizing, and the end-level analytics give great feedback on your performance. There are even mini-games sprinkled in for variety.

This is one of the most complete VR fitness experiences available — polished, motivating, and fun. Highly recommended!

developer_response.body: You summed it up perfectly, Billy’s energy, the mix of moves, and the progression are exactly what make the game tick. Great to hear the feedback system and variety are landing the way we hoped. We’ve got more coming, so it should only get stronger from here.

reviews.review_title: A fun, sweaty blast from the past
reviews.review_description: As the owner of the original boxed set of 3 VHS  Taebo workout videos and no working VHS player anymore I was so excited to see this come to VR so I can continue the fitness journey I started with Billy years ago.  I was so excited when i went into the training dummy room for the first time and saw those original videos playing on the TV in the room it brought back memories. I am loving the variety and different levels.  Taebo was my alltime favorite workout back in the day because it is fun and it works and anyone of any fitness level can do it, and im glad to say that the taebo VR reboot version did not disappoint.  It is more gamified than the  traditional taebo workouts but it is still a good sweaty workout.  Billy will walk you through the basic moves and then they will show you how these work in the game in the fundamentals campaign levels then you can work though campaigns with different intensity levels or just jump in and try yourself in the hardest intensity , work out on a heavy or speed bag, or target training.  The workout can be done in VR or mixed reality.

developer_response.body:
Wow, thank you so much for this amazing review! We love hearing from longtime Tae Bo fans who remember the VHS days, bringing that energy and nostalgia into VR has been such a special part of this project. We’re glad the gym room brought back memories for you.

Billy and the team really wanted Taebo Reboot to be both a fun, sweaty blast and a serious workout, so it means a lot to know it’s hitting the mark. Thanks for being part of this new chapter in the Tae Bo journey!

reviews.review_description: Taebo reboot takes me back to the 90s commercials with all the nostalgia from the comfort of your home with the great graphics and visuals of the vr . You are sure to work up a sweat while having fun with this workout. Here are some reasons why I think this is app is great .you have billy blanks has your coach , you get 100 challenges and 5 elemental books and you can do them at your own pace the tutorials are great,you can focus on your training in the practice pack . This is an amazing full body work out where if you're like me you will be feeling it in the morning. With that being said there is so much more to this  from music to background this app to billy banks speaking this app takes everything to the next level of vr fitness and beyond.

developer_response.body: Love how you tied it back to the 90s vibe, that nostalgia with Billy as your coach is a big part of what makes this special. Hearing you’re feeling it the next morning means the workouts are doing their job. Thanks for laying out all the details so clear 🙏

reviews.review_description:
I have been looking out for a good boxing based workout (something that wasn't primarily a rhythm game) and this definitely ticks that box!

With its ability based training plans, each having multiple stages, this is a great offering into the VR Fitness genre. Each stage gives you a video guide as to what is expected and a goal for you to hit. The stages are nice and short but stacked together, really hit the spot for a quality workout. If you just fancy getting those arms moving, then the practice areas have you covered. It also supports MR for those times when you are worried about making contact with walls or TVs.

There are way too many positives to go through in a review, so I would definitely recommend checking this one out.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for this! Really glad the training plans and short stacked stages are hitting the spot, that balance was a big focus for us. Awesome to hear you’re using both the practice areas and MR mode too. Appreciate the recommendation!

reviews.review_description: This game is amazing, you get the Tae Bo experience in a really challenging and fun game and the fundamentals pack properly teaches you all the moves needed for the more advanced elemental book challenges, this game really makes you sweat and burn calories, would totally recommend to anyone who is into fitness.

developer_response.body: Right on, the fundamentals were built to set up those harder book challenges, so it’s awesome to hear they worked the way we hoped. Glad it’s giving you that real sweat and calorie burn. Thanks for recommending it!

reviews.review_description:
There are a lot of workout apps in VR. Some are good, many are not. Taebo Reboot is one that stands out amongst the crowd for several reasons. First, this is based on the Taebo exercise videos from the past and you get that same sense of intensity in this modern VR reboot version. Depending on which intensity level you choose, the workouts can get your extreme calorie burn kicked into ultra high gear. The mechanics are similar where each glove is a different color and you have to hit the colored punching pads with the right glove at the right angle (ie. Jab, hook, uppercut). But there are also walls you have a dodge, low walls you have to jump over, and spike balls and bombs that you have to dodge. The targets also come from different directions (front, 45 degrees left and 45 degrees right). And to really up your eye-hand coordination, some of the punching pads will also move left/right and up/down which will force you to time your punches location accordingly.

After each training session, there are stats that show your punch strength by arm which is something I haven’t seen before. Apparently, my right arm is great for jabs and hooks and my left is significantly better at uppercuts. It gives you an added level of analysis to help you think where your form and strength needs improvement. In fact, I think this might be the best feature of ANY VR workout game!

There are multiple “books” (ie. Different environments, training intensities, each containing 20+ workouts each), including a tutorial where Billy Blanks walks you through the mechanics of each move being introduced.

There’s also a speed bag drill where you have to hit the bad into targets/coins which means that besides speed, you also have to focus on precision so that you can hit the bag in the right direction. There’s also a heavy bag and a human dummy to test your power punches (hit targets that pop up on the bag/dummy).

There’s a ton of variety so I don’t think you’ll ever get bored. It’s really good, get it!

developer_response.body: Wow, thanks for such a detailed breakdown! You really captured what we tried to build, intensity, variety, and tools that actually help you understand your own progress. Hearing that the stat tracking stood out means a lot, that feature was a big focus for us. We’ve got more updates and content in the works, so hopefully it keeps surprising you.

reviews.review_title: Taebo Reboot: Super Fun VR Workout!
reviews.review_description:
What I really liked about the game:
The music and graphics were better than I expected. The environments range from a Japanese garden to a volcano, making the experience lively and engaging. The gameplay is energetic and varied. You train not only your body but also your focus and precision. It’s a fun way to stay fit.

Small problems:
A small issue is that some movements can be a bit confusing at first, and it takes a moment to get used to the hand tracking.

Is the game worth the price?
Yes, the game is worth the $19.99 price. For this price, you get access to over 100 workouts, various environments, and a motivating experience with Billy Blanks.

Conclusion:
Taebo Reboot is an energetic and motivating VR fitness game that challenges you to get stronger both physically and mentally. With guidance from Billy Blanks and a variety of environments, it’s a fun way to stay active. If you’re looking for an interactive and challenging workout, this is definitely recommended.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the great feedback! It's nice to see that you’re enjoying the workouts, music, and environments. Hope you keep having fun training with Billy!

reviews.review_description:
This game explains pretty well the basics of how to box, and gives you some physical training. Really well done in my opinion, if you do everything the way he says, you will feel your muscles burn the next day. It's also gonna improve your stamina, because it's basically like cardio training, because boxing is really exhausting. Music is good, environments are good. I honestly didn't really go there for the music, I was focused on learning how to punch.
The game menus look too cartoon-ish but it's a fix for the future updates I guess.

developer_response.body: “Exactly, do the moves the way Billy shows and you feel it the next day. That burn and cardio kick is what makes boxing so effective. Thanks for pointing out the menus too, updates will smooth that out.

reviews.review_description:
Did you grow up in the 90’s? If you did it’s highly likely you would remember Billy Blanks and Taebo being absolutely everywhere. Well he’s back in 2025 and ready to get you sweating up a storm in VR. 

This take’s a formula that we have seen plenty before combining music and moving targets that you hit so score points, the difference here is they are teaching you technique to use and intensity to go alongside those aspects and that’s where this one rises above the rest to deliver a quality fitness app. Start on the basics and work your work through levels, sculptured to deliver different  visual experiences.

From the start this delivers a solid fun experience and a serious workout. I really enjoyed the core gameplay, the music and the visuals and this is where the game shines but there are still some rough edges here through the menus and a few interactions just don’t feel great yet but I feel with a little polish these should be an easy fix. Overall you are here for the meat of the game and that’s where it shines so I would Highly recommend this if you are after a fun and effective fitness app. 

User warning have a towel ready, you ARE going to swear up a storm.

A shoutout to Impact Labs for inviting me participate in the playtest and helping to give feedback for the this app.

developer_response.body: Really appreciate you taking the time to share this! Happy to hear our VR version is bringing back that classic Tae Bo sweat and fun. Totally agree, towel required. We know there are still a few rough spots in the menus and interactions, and we’re working on smoothing those out. Thanks again for jumping in.
